response: This implements the classic **triple-nested-loop matrix multiplication**: it computes each entry \(C[i][j]\) of the result as the dot product \(\sum_{k=0}^{n-1} A[i][k]\cdot B[k][j]\), where \(A\) is \(m \times n\) and \(B\) is \(n \times p\). It initializes an \(m \times p\) zero matrix and fills it by accumulating products in the innermost loop.
